rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN M BARISH et al VS. STEVEN D BROOKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainant Steven Brook'S Motion To Compel Jay Morton To Respond To Interrogatories And Demand For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 23, CROSS-COMPLAINANT STEVEN BROOK'S MOTION TO COMPEL JAY MORTON TO RESPOND TO INTERROGATORIES AND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Huibonhoa as follows: Grant the motion to compel and award sanctions in the amount requested $1,635, to be paid within 30 days. The motion is properly heard in the discovery department. There is no order requiring that the motion be heard by Judge Karnow. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE BROWN & PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DINESH MANIAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Far East National Bank Motion To Compel The Depo And Production Of Docs (Joinder)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 10, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK's (Joinder) Motion To Compel The Deposition And Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH ACCESS VS. DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motoin To Enforce The Judgment And Writ
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 11, PETITIONER MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH ACCESS' Amended Notice Of MotIOn To Enforce The Judgment And Writ. Respondents must implement the new screening process by August 31, 2012 and must continue with that process unless and until the writ is modified or dissolved due to new or conflicting federal or state legislation or otherwise. The request for premium reimbursements is denied without prejudice to making a formal request for such reimbursements. Petitioner has not cited any authority empowering this court to make such an order in the context of an opposition to a return to a writ when the writ is silent on the issue. Petitioner's request for a further order regarding deemed eligible infants is denied without prejudice to filing a supplemental writ on this issue. The hearing is continued to September 6, 2012 for compliance review. Respondents must file a status report no later than September 4, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 24, DEFENDANT JEFFREY LEVIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to provide courtesy copies of his opposition papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B and the tentative ruling issued in advance of the hearing on July 2, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARY MCFADDEN VS. JENTA SHEN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 18, DEFENDANT JENTA SHEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Ms. McFadden has adequately pled a claim of medical battery for exceeding conditional consent. That claim is not uncertain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. RMF ENGINEERING, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-518111 WITH CASE CGC-12-518341
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 19, PLAINTIFF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-518111 WITH CASE CGC-12-518341. Granted in large part. No opposition filed. Case number 518111 is consolidated for all purposes with case number 518341. Case number 518111 is the lead case and all further filings will be in that case only. The August 29, 2012 case management conference in case number 518341 is vacated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Reclassification
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 20, PLAINTIFF PACIFIC RESOURCES STEVENSON, INC.'S Motion For Reclassification. Granted. No opposition filed. rulings.Case Title: ELLEN PAO VS. KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 21, DEFENDANT KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration. Denied. There is no arbitration agreement between plaintiff and defendant. Defendant's argument raised for the first time in its reply papers that it is a third-party beneficiary of the Managing LLC Agreements which permits it to enforce the arbitration provisions of those agreements was not considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PATRICIA GIBBS VS. MARTHA MOHR DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Martha Mohr Davis' Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Request For Permanent Restrains Set To Be Heard May-30-12
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2011, Line 25, DEFENDANT MARTHA MOHR DAVIS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) REQUEST FOR PERMANENT RESTRAINTS. Continued on the court's own motion to July 19, 2012 at 9:30 to permit Ms. Davis time to file a substantive reply to Ms. Gibbs' opposition late-filed and served on June 29, 2012 and provide the parties an opportunity to correct the deficiencies in the courtesy copies previously submitted. Any further reply papers shall be filed no later than July 13, 2012 and courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 at the time of filing. The parties are to re-submit courtesy copies of all moving papers and opposition papers to Department 302 no later than 4pm July 11, 2012. The courtesy copies must be in compliance with CRC 3.1110(c), (e) and (f) and accompanied by a cover letter stating the new hearing date. =(302/HEK)
